element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Weapons now have limited uses before breaking; character-specific integration with unique moves
All characters can now run (advancement from SoR2); dodge roll essential for environmental hazards
Upgradeable through successive play; weapon-integrated special attacks follow same system
Allows full-power attacks without health loss when meter is full; resource management mechanic
Originally featured in Streets of Rage 1; requires spatial awareness and dodge mechanics
Conditional paths based on player actions (e.g., clearing rooms before NPC death from poisonous gas)
Enemies pick up weapons, block attacks, employ co-operative tactics, steal food items to regain health
RoboCy Corporation robots; culminates in final confrontation with Robot Y and Mr. X's brain
Affects which of four possible endings player receives; creates time pressure for optimal playthrough

typical_tension_triggers: NPC survival conditions with time limits
Bomb timer pressure in later stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension with faster gameplay; wave-based enemy spawning punctuated by resource scarcity and environmental pressure; story-driven mid-game shift from street combat to corporate/facility infiltration creates narrative pacing layer

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Alternate stage routes conditional on clearing enemy waves or protecting NPCs; unlockable character access through fulfilling gameplay conditions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden characters (Shiva, Roo/Victy, Ash in JP version) unlocked by meeting specific stage conditions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat, movement options, and weapon systems; safe learning environment for core mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Environmental hazards introduced; conditional level routes add complexity; enemy AI escalates with blocking and cooperation; time-pressure mechanics emerge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Maximum enemy AI difficulty; hazard density increases; bomb timer pressure; final confrontation with robot army and Mr. X's brain; Western release features increased difficulty over JP version
notable_spikes: Introduction of poisonous gas rooms with NPC survival conditions
Stage transitions involving robot facility infiltration
Final stage robot Y and Mr. X brain battle
Western release overall difficulty increase vs. Japanese version

notable_level_patterns.description: Up to two players fight successive waves of enemies; patterns scale in complexity and AI sophistication as game progresses
Stage paths branch based on player actions, such as clearing rooms quickly enough to prevent NPC death from environmental threats
Weapon-limited encounters (weapons break after limited uses); food scarcity creates health-management tension; Special attack meter fills through play
Bottomless pits and poisonous gas introduced in mid-game; requires mastery of dodge roll and spatial awareness; tied to time-pressure objectives
Four possible endings based on difficulty setting and timed stage completion; true ending involves robot factory infiltration and final Mr. X confrontation; cutscenes add story depth
